field_a: Extreme value statistics (GEV distributions, tail index analysis, return level estimation, peaks-over-threshold)
field_c: Proteome-wide thermal stability distributions (thermal proteome profiling, Meltome Atlas)

hypotheses.title: GEV Tail Index Classifies Thermal Adaptation Strategies Across the Tree of Life
hypotheses.connection: Extreme value statistics → GEV shape parameter ξ → Evolutionary thermal adaptation strategy classification
hypotheses.key_prediction: ξ correlates negatively with OGT across 13 Meltome Atlas species; thermophiles have more negative ξ (Weibull, truncated tail) than psychrophiles (approaching Gumbel); expected difference 0.3-0.5
grounded_claims: Meltome Atlas data (Jarzab 2020, Nature Methods, PMID 32284610)
Thermophile amino acid adaptations (documented in structural biology)
hypotheses.why_wrong: Proteome composition (IDP fraction, membrane protein fraction) may dominate tail shape over thermal adaptation; 13 species with phylogenetic confounding (archaea-eukaryote split); OGT range may be insufficient
hypotheses.literature_gap: No study has fitted GEV to proteome Tm distributions or classified organisms by extreme value domain of attraction

hypotheses.title: Complex-Minimum Tm Return Levels Predict Process-Specific Thermal Failure Temperatures
hypotheses.connection: Extreme value statistics → Return levels on complex-minimum Tm → Pathway-specific thermal failure prediction
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Return level estimation at complex-bottleneck level
hypotheses.key_prediction: 1% return level of ribosomal complex-minimum Tm predicts temperature of 90% translation loss (±2°C); same for respiratory chain and Seahorse-measured O2 consumption drop
parametric_claims: Mapping to puromycin incorporation / Seahorse assays
hypotheses.why_wrong: Chaperones may rescue bottleneck subunits above in-vitro Tm; kinetic effects (heating rate, exposure duration) not captured by equilibrium Tm; subunit redundancy in some complexes
hypotheses.literature_gap: No framework predicts temperature of SPECIFIC process failure with quantified uncertainty; Jarzab 2020 noted mitochondrial respiration at 46°C but as anecdotal observation

hypotheses.title: POT Analysis Reveals Intrinsically Disordered Proteins as the Functional Architecture of Thermal Vulnerability
hypotheses.connection: Extreme value statistics → POT/GPD exceedance set → Functional profiling of thermally vulnerable subproteome
hypotheses.key_prediction: GPD-defined exceedance set enriched >2x for IDPs (>30% disorder), signaling GO terms, and STRING hub proteins (degree >10); bimodal ξ in IDP vs non-IDP exceedances
grounded_claims: IDP structural instability (established in protein science)
hypotheses.why_wrong: Protein molecular weight confounds both disorder and Tm variability; most vulnerable IDPs may be censored (Tm < 30°C); STRING degree enrichment may reflect annotation bias
hypotheses.literature_gap: TPP uses arbitrary Tm cutoffs (bottom 5-10%) without EVT-principled threshold selection; no study has used mean residual life plot for proteome threshold definition

hypotheses.title: Censored GEV Estimation Recovers the Invisible Proteome and Quantifies Systematic Vulnerability Bias
hypotheses.connection: Extreme value statistics → Censored GEV likelihood → Corrected proteome Tm distribution with predicted unmeasured values
hypotheses.key_prediction: Censored vs naive GEV: Δμ > 1°C downward, Δσ > 0.5°C increase; left-censored proteins enriched for IDPs and chaperone clients; validated within ±3°C against extended-range TPP
grounded_claims: Censored extreme value methods (standard in hydrology, Stedinger et al. 1993 Handbook of Hydrology)
20% unmeasured proteome (Jarzab 2020; Figueroa-Navedo 2024, Cell Reports Methods)
hypotheses.why_wrong: Proteins with Tm < 30°C may not have cooperative unfolding (IDPs unfold non-cooperatively → 'Tm' undefined); right-censored may include aggregation-resistant peptides; informative censoring violates MLE independence
hypotheses.literature_gap: Figueroa-Navedo 2024 explicitly flags 20% problem as unresolved; GPMelt addresses curve fitting NOT censoring; censored EVT is the exact statistical framework for detection-limit data

hypotheses.title: The Extremal Index Encodes Proteome Modularity and Chaperone Buffering Capacity
hypotheses.connection: Extreme value statistics → Extremal index θ → Co-aggregation cascade length and chaperone network effectiveness
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: Extremal index θ (dependence structure of extremes)
hypotheses.key_prediction: θ ~ 0.5-0.7 for human proteome (cascade size 1.4-2.0); membrane θ < soluble θ; θ positively correlates with chaperone-to-client ratio across 13 species
hypotheses.why_wrong: In-vitro lysate Tm may not reflect in-vivo co-aggregation (diluted chaperones, lost compartmentalization); θ estimation assumes exchangeable observations (questionable for diverse proteome); expression-level confounding
hypotheses.literature_gap: TPCA reports pairwise co-aggregation but no system-level metric exists; extremal index provides exactly this — single number for proteome-wide co-aggregation propensity

hypotheses.title: Fisher-Tippett-Gnedenko Universality Guarantees Weibull-Domain Convergence for All Proteome Tm Distributions
hypotheses.connection: Extreme value statistics → FTG theorem → Universal Weibull prediction for bounded proteome Tm extremes
hypotheses.key_prediction: All 13 species accept Weibull (ξ < 0) at p > 0.05 by Anderson-Darling/KS tests; fitted endpoint ω correlates with OGT; no Gumbel or Fréchet behavior
grounded_claims: FTG theorem (Fisher & Tippett 1928, Proc Cambridge Phil Soc; Gnedenko 1943, Annals of Mathematics)
parametric_claims: Block sizes sufficient for asymptotic convergence
hypotheses.why_wrong: FTG is asymptotic — blocks of 10-30 proteins per pathway may not reach convergence; IDPs without cooperative unfolding reduce effective block sizes; 90°C ceiling is measurement artifact not biophysical limit
hypotheses.literature_gap: No study has tested which extreme value domain proteome Tm distributions belong to; FTG theorem (85+ years old) never applied to proteomics

hypotheses.title: GPD Scale Parameter Predicts Evolutionary Rate in the Thermally Vulnerable Subproteome
hypotheses.connection: Extreme value statistics → GPD σ of lower-tail exceedances → Purifying selection strength (dN/dS) on vulnerable proteins
hypotheses.bridge_mechanism: GPD scale parameter σ as evolutionary constraint metric
hypotheses.key_prediction: σ negatively correlates with dN/dS in tail-protein orthologs across 13 species; small σ = tight constraint = slower evolution; controls for expression level and protein size
grounded_claims: dN/dS estimation (Yang 2007, PAML, Mol Biol Evol)
parametric_claims: Narrow vulnerability zone imposes stronger purifying selection
Effect detectable after controlling for confounders
hypotheses.why_wrong: dN/dS dominated by expression level, interaction degree, essentiality, population size; σ-dN/dS correlation may be undetectable; tail protein identity differs across species making ortholog comparison difficult
hypotheses.literature_gap: Evolutionary rate of thermally vulnerable proteins never analyzed as a class; GPD provides first principled definition of 'thermally vulnerable subproteome' for evolutionary comparison

downgraded_details: Smith 1985 censored GEV → replaced with Stedinger et al. 1993 Handbook of Hydrology (more defensible)
~120°C upper Tm bound → flagged as PARAMETRIC
directionality_verified: H1: more negative ξ = shorter tail = thermophiles ✓
H2: return level R_p increases with p ✓
H5: more chaperones → higher θ (prevents cascades) ✓
quantitative_checks: H2: ±2°C aggressive but consistent with Jarzab 2020 (mitochondria functional at 46°C, bulk Tm ~50°C)
H5: θ ~ 0.5-0.7 consistent with 350+ complexes in 13K protein proteome (60% in complexes, ~5 subunits avg)
self_critique.bridge_diversity: 7 hypotheses × 7 distinct bridge mechanisms — no duplication (constraint satisfied: ≤2 per bridge)
self_critique.multi_level_abstraction: H2 and H5 both have 4-level abstraction (molecular, systemic, formal, informational) — meets ≥2 requirement

target_evaluator_concerns_addressed.phenomenological_risk: All 7 pair EVT with biological predictions (adaptation strategies, pathway failure, disorder enrichment, invisible proteome, modularity, universality, evolutionary rate)
target_evaluator_concerns_addressed.independence_violation: H2 uses complex-minimum Tm; H5 models dependence as biological signal via extremal index
target_evaluator_concerns_addressed.censoring: H4 directly addresses with censored GEV methodology
target_evaluator_concerns_addressed.mechanistic_depth: H2, H3, H5, H7 include mechanism-level predictions beyond statistical description

computational_validator_recommendations_: Thermophile xi more negative than mesophile (H1) ✓
Complex-min return levels predict process failure (H2) ✓
BLAST Gumbel NOT included as prior work ✓
